option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Architecture & Test Scenarios
## Component Architecture
### 1. Model (`internal/model/`)
The central data model is a pure Go representation of AWS policy constructs. All other components speak this language.
``` Principal ├── ID: string (ARN) ├── Type: Role | User | Group | Service └── Policies: []Policy
Policy ├── ID: string (ARN or name) ├── Type: Identity | Resource | SCP | PermissionBoundary | Session └── Statements: []Statement
Statement ├── SID: string ├── Effect: Allow | Deny ├── Actions: []string ("s3:GetObject", "s3:*") ├── Resources: []string (ARNs, "*") ├── Principals: []string (ARNs, "*") — for resource-based policies └── Conditions: map[string]Condition
Resource (S3) ├── ID: string (ARN) ├── BucketName: string └── BucketPolicy: *Policy
AccessQuery ├── PrincipalARN: string ├── ResourceARN: string └── Action: string
AccessResult ├── Decision: Allow | Deny | ImplicitDeny ├── DeniedAtLayer: int (1-7, 0 if allowed) ├── DenyingStatement: *Statement └── LayerResults: []LayerResult ```
### 2. Parser (`internal/parser/`)
Reads HCL Terraform files using `github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2` and `github.com/zclconf/go-cty`.
**Supported resource types:**
| Terraform Resource | Maps To | |---|---| | `aws_iam_role` | `Principal{Type: Role}` | | `aws_iam_user` | `Principal{Type: User}` | | `aws_iam_policy` | `Policy{Type: Identity}` | | `aws_iam_role_policy` (inline) | `Policy{Type: Identity}` (inline) | | `aws_iam_role_policy_attachment` | links Principal → Policy | | `aws_iam_policy_attachment` | links Principal → Policy | | `aws_s3_bucket` | `Resource` | | `aws_s3_bucket_policy` | `Policy{Type: Resource}` | | `aws_organizations_policy` | `Policy{Type: SCP}` | | `aws_iam_role` (permissions_boundary) | `Policy{Type: PermissionBoundary}` |
**Parser output:** a `ParsedState` struct containing slices of principals and resources with all policies resolved and cross-references linked.
**Parsing strategy:** 1. Walk all `.tf` files in input directory 2. Collect all resource blocks by type into a registry 3. Second pass: resolve references (e.g., `aws_iam_role_policy_attachment` links role ARN to policy ARN) 4. Inline JSON policy documents are parsed with `encoding/json`
### 3. Transformer (`internal/transformer/`)
Converts `ParsedState` → an Alloy specification string (`.als` file content).
**Alloy Specification Structure:**
```alloy -- Generated by access-control-helper --
module s3_access_control
-- Core signatures abstract sig Effect {} one sig Allow, Deny extends Effect {}
abstract sig Action {} -- S3 actions instantiated from parsed data one sig GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject extends Action {}
abstract sig Principal {} -- Principals instantiated from Terraform state one sig ExampleRole extends Principal {}
abstract sig Resource {} one sig MyBucket extends Resource {}
sig Statement { effect: one Effect, actions: set Action, resources: set Resource, principals: set Principal }
sig Policy { ptype: one PolicyType, statements: set Statement }
abstract sig PolicyType {} one sig IdentityBased, ResourceBased, SCP, PermBoundary, SessionPolicy extends PolicyType {}
-- Facts: which policies are attached to which principals/resources fact Attachments { ExampleRole.policies = IdentityPolicy1 MyBucket.policy = BucketPolicy1 }
-- Predicates: layer-by-layer evaluation pred explicitDeny[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... } pred scpAllows[p: Principal, a: Action] { ... } pred bucketPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... } pred permBoundaryAllows[p: Principal, a: Action] { ... } pred identityPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... }
pred canAccess[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { not explicitDeny[p, r, a] scpAllows[p, a] (bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a] or (permBoundaryAllows[p, a] and identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a])) }
-- Assertions to check assert ExampleRoleCanGetObject { canAccess[ExampleRole, MyBucket, GetObject] } check ExampleRoleCanGetObject ```
**Transformer design notes:** - Each unique action, principal, and resource from Terraform becomes an Alloy `one sig` - ARNs are sanitized to valid Alloy identifiers (alphanumeric, no colons/slashes) - Wildcard actions (`s3:*`) expand to all known S3 actions - Resource wildcards (`arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*`) are resolved against known resources
### 4. Analyzer (`internal/analyzer/`)
Runs the Alloy CLI and parses its output.
**Alloy invocation:** ``` alloy execute --check spec.als ```
Alloy returns: - `No counterexample found` → assertion holds → access decision confirmed - `Counterexample found` + instance → assertion violated → access denied, instance shows which statement
**Output parsing:** - Parse Alloy XML/text output - Map counterexample back to originating Terraform statement - Populate `AccessResult.DenyingStatement`
**Fallback:** if the Alloy CLI is not installed, the analyzer can optionally run Alloy via its Java API using `os/exec` with the Alloy JAR.
### 5. Reporter (`internal/reporter/`)
Takes `[]AccessResult` and renders them to stdout (default), JSON, or SARIF (for CI integration).
---
## Alloy Model Design (Core)
The hardest part is encoding AWS's policy evaluation correctly. Key rules:
### Explicit Deny (Layer 1) ```alloy pred explicitDenyExists[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { some s: Statement | s.effect = Deny and (p in s.principals or AWS_STAR in s.principals) and (a in s.actions or STAR_ACTION in s.actions) and (r in s.resources or STAR_RESOURCE in s.resources) } ```
### Bucket Policy Cross-Account Rule Bucket policy can grant access to a principal from *another* account directly, bypassing the need for an identity-based policy. This is modeled as: ```alloy pred bucketPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { some s: r.policy.statements | s.effect = Allow and (p in s.principals or AWS_STAR in s.principals) and (a in s.actions or STAR_ACTION in s.actions) } ```
### Identity + Boundary conjunction (Layers 4+6) ```alloy pred identityPathAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a] and (no p.permBoundary or permBoundaryAllows[p, r, a]) } ```
### Final access predicate ```alloy pred canAccess[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { not explicitDenyExists[p, r, a] and (bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a] or identityPathAllows[p, r, a]) } ```
---
## Test Scenarios
### Scenario 1 — Simple Allow via IAM Policy
**Setup:** - IAM Role `app-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:GetObject` on `my-bucket` - S3 bucket `my-bucket` with no bucket policy
**Expected:** ALLOW at Layer 6 (identity policy)
**Terraform fixture:** ```hcl resource "aws_iam_role" "app_role" { name = "app-role" ... }
resource "aws_iam_policy" "s3_read" { policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17" Statement = [{ Effect = "Allow" Action = ["s3:GetObject"] Resource = "arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/*" }] }) }
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "attach" { role = aws_iam_role.app_role.name policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.s3_read.arn }
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "my_bucket" { bucket = "my-bucket" } ```
---
### Scenario 2 — Explicit Deny in Bucket Policy overrides IAM Allow
**Setup:** - IAM Role `app-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:*` - Bucket policy with explicit Deny on `s3:DeleteObject` for all principals
**Expected:** DENY at Layer 1 for `s3:DeleteObject`, ALLOW for `s3:GetObject`
**Key assertion:** ```alloy assert AppRoleCannotDelete { not canAccess[AppRole, MyBucket, DeleteObject] } check AppRoleCannotDelete ```
---
### Scenario 3 — Access Blocked by Missing Permission Boundary
**Setup:** - IAM Role `restricted-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:*` - Permission boundary on `restricted-role` that only allows `s3:GetObject`
**Expected:** DENY for `s3:PutObject` (blocked at Layer 4), ALLOW for `s3:GetObject`
**Key point:** identity policy alone is insufficient; both identity policy AND boundary must allow.
---
### Scenario 4 — Bucket Policy Grants Cross-Account Access (No IAM Policy Needed)
**Setup:** - IAM Role `cross-account-role` in account `111111111111` - Bucket in account `222222222222` - Bucket policy explicitly allows `cross-account-role`'s ARN
**Expected:** ALLOW via Layer 3 (bucket policy) — identity-based policy not required for same-account resources.
---
### Scenario 5 — SCP Blocks Access Org-Wide
**Setup:** - AWS Organization SCP denying `s3:DeleteBucket` for all principals - IAM Role with full `s3:*` identity policy
**Expected:** DENY at Layer 2 for `s3:DeleteBucket`
---
### Scenario 6 — Wildcard Resource in Policy
**Setup:** - IAM policy with `Resource = "*"` allowing `s3:GetObject` - Query against a specific bucket
**Expected:** ALLOW (wildcard matches all buckets)
**Test note:** transformer must expand `"*"` to include all known `Resource` sigs in the Alloy spec.
---
### Scenario 7 — Implicit Deny (No Policy Covers the Action)
**Setup:** - IAM Role with no policies attached - S3 bucket with no bucket policy
**Expected:** IMPLICIT DENY (no Allow found anywhere)
---
### Scenario 8 — Inline Policy vs Managed Policy
**Setup:** - `aws_iam_role_policy` (inline) and `aws_iam_policy` + attachment (managed) on same role - Inline policy allows `s3:GetObject`, managed policy allows `s3:PutObject`
**Expected:** both actions ALLOW (union of all attached policies)
---
## Requirements & Test Scenarios: Per-Action Access Evaluation
The system must: - For every (principal, bucket, action) triple, evaluate access according to all AWS S3 policy evaluation steps (explicit Deny, RCP, SCP, resource-based, identity-based, permission boundaries, session policies). - Correctly model explicit Deny and Allow statements from Terraform policies, including Deny for specific actions (e.g., s3:DeleteObject) and Allow for others (e.g., s3:GetObject). - Output, for each query, the final access decision (ALLOW or DENY) and the evaluation layer responsible (e.g., DENY at Layer 1 due to explicit Deny in bucket policy). - Generate Alloy assertions for every (principal, bucket, action) triple, so that Alloy checks confirm the tool's reasoning for each action.
### Example Test Scenario
Given a Terraform file with a bucket policy that Denies s3:DeleteObject and Allows s3:GetObject for a principal: - The generated Alloy model must DENY DeleteObject at Layer 1 (explicit Deny) and ALLOW GetObject if it passes all layers. - The Alloy output must show which layer made the decision and why, matching the AWS evaluation logic.
---
## Suggested Implementation Order
``` Phase 1 — Foundation [x] Data model (model.go) [ ] Terraform parser for aws_iam_role, aws_iam_policy, aws_s3_bucket [ ] Basic Alloy template generation (no conditions)
Phase 2 — Core Analysis [ ] Full policy attachment resolution [ ] Alloy spec with all 7 evaluation layers [ ] Alloy CLI integration & output parsing
Phase 3 — Coverage [ ] IAM conditions support (StringEquals, ArnLike, etc.) [ ] aws_organizations_policy (SCP) parsing [ ] Permission boundary parsing [ ] Cross-account analysis
Phase 4 — UX [ ] JSON and SARIF output [ ] CI mode (exit code 1 on DENY findings) [ ] Example fixtures and integration tests ```
---
## Dependencies to Add to go.mod
``` github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2 v2.19.1 # HCL parsing github.com/zclconf/go-cty v1.14.1 # HCL value types github.com/hashicorp/go-multierror v1.1.1 # error aggregation github.com/stretchr/testify v1.9.0 # test assertions ```

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Architecture & Test Scenarios
## Component Architecture
### 1. Model (`internal/model/`)
The central data model is a pure Go representation of AWS policy constructs. All other components speak this language.
``` Principal ├── ID: string (ARN) ├── Type: Role | User | Group | Service └── Policies: []Policy
Policy ├── ID: string (ARN or name) ├── Type: Identity | Resource | SCP | PermissionBoundary | Session └── Statements: []Statement
Statement ├── SID: string ├── Effect: Allow | Deny ├── Actions: []string ("s3:GetObject", "s3:*") ├── Resources: []string (ARNs, "*") ├── Principals: []string (ARNs, "*") — for resource-based policies └── Conditions: map[string]Condition
Resource (S3) ├── ID: string (ARN) ├── BucketName: string └── BucketPolicy: *Policy
AccessQuery ├── PrincipalARN: string ├── ResourceARN: string └── Action: string
AccessResult ├── Decision: Allow | Deny | ImplicitDeny ├── DeniedAtLayer: int (1-7, 0 if allowed) ├── DenyingStatement: *Statement └── LayerResults: []LayerResult ```
### 2. Parser (`internal/parser/`)
Reads HCL Terraform files using `github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2` and `github.com/zclconf/go-cty`.
**Supported resource types:**
| Terraform Resource | Maps To | |---|---| | `aws_iam_role` | `Principal{Type: Role}` | | `aws_iam_user` | `Principal{Type: User}` | | `aws_iam_policy` | `Policy{Type: Identity}` | | `aws_iam_role_policy` (inline) | `Policy{Type: Identity}` (inline) | | `aws_iam_role_policy_attachment` | links Principal → Policy | | `aws_iam_policy_attachment` | links Principal → Policy | | `aws_s3_bucket` | `Resource` | | `aws_s3_bucket_policy` | `Policy{Type: Resource}` | | `aws_organizations_policy` | `Policy{Type: SCP}` | | `aws_iam_role` (permissions_boundary) | `Policy{Type: PermissionBoundary}` |
**Parser output:** a `ParsedState` struct containing slices of principals and resources with all policies resolved and cross-references linked.
**Parsing strategy:** 1. Walk all `.tf` files in input directory 2. Collect all resource blocks by type into a registry 3. Second pass: resolve references (e.g., `aws_iam_role_policy_attachment` links role ARN to policy ARN) 4. Inline JSON policy documents are parsed with `encoding/json`
### 3. Transformer (`internal/transformer/`)
Converts `ParsedState` → an Alloy specification string (`.als` file content).
**Alloy Specification Structure:**
```alloy -- Generated by access-control-helper --
module s3_access_control
-- Core signatures abstract sig Effect {} one sig Allow, Deny extends Effect {}
abstract sig Action {} -- S3 actions instantiated from parsed data one sig GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject extends Action {}
abstract sig Principal {} -- Principals instantiated from Terraform state one sig ExampleRole extends Principal {}
abstract sig Resource {} one sig MyBucket extends Resource {}
sig Statement { effect: one Effect, actions: set Action, resources: set Resource, principals: set Principal }
sig Policy { ptype: one PolicyType, statements: set Statement }
abstract sig PolicyType {} one sig IdentityBased, ResourceBased, SCP, PermBoundary, SessionPolicy extends PolicyType {}
-- Facts: which policies are attached to which principals/resources fact Attachments { ExampleRole.policies = IdentityPolicy1 MyBucket.policy = BucketPolicy1 }
-- Predicates: layer-by-layer evaluation pred explicitDeny[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... } pred scpAllows[p: Principal, a: Action] { ... } pred bucketPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... } pred permBoundaryAllows[p: Principal, a: Action] { ... } pred identityPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { ... }
pred canAccess[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { not explicitDeny[p, r, a] scpAllows[p, a] (bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a] or (permBoundaryAllows[p, a] and identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a])) }
-- Assertions to check assert ExampleRoleCanGetObject { canAccess[ExampleRole, MyBucket, GetObject] } check ExampleRoleCanGetObject ```
**Transformer design notes:** - Each unique action, principal, and resource from Terraform becomes an Alloy `one sig` - ARNs are sanitized to valid Alloy identifiers (alphanumeric, no colons/slashes) - Wildcard actions (`s3:*`) expand to all known S3 actions - Resource wildcards (`arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*`) are resolved against known resources
### 4. Analyzer (`internal/analyzer/`)
Runs the Alloy CLI and parses its output.
**Alloy invocation:** ``` alloy execute --check spec.als ```
Alloy returns: - `No counterexample found` → assertion holds → access decision confirmed - `Counterexample found` + instance → assertion violated → access denied, instance shows which statement
**Output parsing:** - Parse Alloy XML/text output - Map counterexample back to originating Terraform statement - Populate `AccessResult.DenyingStatement`
**Fallback:** if the Alloy CLI is not installed, the analyzer can optionally run Alloy via its Java API using `os/exec` with the Alloy JAR.
### 5. Reporter (`internal/reporter/`)
Takes `[]AccessResult` and renders them to stdout (default), JSON, or SARIF (for CI integration).
---
## Alloy Model Design (Core)
The hardest part is encoding AWS's policy evaluation correctly. Key rules:
### Explicit Deny (Layer 1) ```alloy pred explicitDenyExists[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { some s: Statement | s.effect = Deny and (p in s.principals or AWS_STAR in s.principals) and (a in s.actions or STAR_ACTION in s.actions) and (r in s.resources or STAR_RESOURCE in s.resources) } ```
### Bucket Policy Cross-Account Rule Bucket policy can grant access to a principal from *another* account directly, bypassing the need for an identity-based policy. This is modeled as: ```alloy pred bucketPolicyAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { some s: r.policy.statements | s.effect = Allow and (p in s.principals or AWS_STAR in s.principals) and (a in s.actions or STAR_ACTION in s.actions) } ```
### Identity + Boundary conjunction (Layers 4+6) ```alloy pred identityPathAllows[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a] and (no p.permBoundary or permBoundaryAllows[p, r, a]) } ```
### Final access predicate ```alloy pred canAccess[p: Principal, r: Resource, a: Action] { not explicitDenyExists[p, r, a] and (bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a] or identityPathAllows[p, r, a]) } ```
---
## Test Scenarios
### Scenario 1 — Simple Allow via IAM Policy
**Setup:** - IAM Role `app-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:GetObject` on `my-bucket` - S3 bucket `my-bucket` with no bucket policy
**Expected:** ALLOW at Layer 6 (identity policy)
**Terraform fixture:** ```hcl resource "aws_iam_role" "app_role" { name = "app-role" ... }
resource "aws_iam_policy" "s3_read" { policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17" Statement = [{ Effect = "Allow" Action = ["s3:GetObject"] Resource = "arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/*" }] }) }
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "attach" { role = aws_iam_role.app_role.name policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.s3_read.arn }
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "my_bucket" { bucket = "my-bucket" } ```
---
### Scenario 2 — Explicit Deny in Bucket Policy overrides IAM Allow
**Setup:** - IAM Role `app-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:*` - Bucket policy with explicit Deny on `s3:DeleteObject` for all principals
**Expected:** DENY at Layer 1 for `s3:DeleteObject`, ALLOW for `s3:GetObject`
**Key assertion:** ```alloy assert AppRoleCannotDelete { not canAccess[AppRole, MyBucket, DeleteObject] } check AppRoleCannotDelete ```
---
### Scenario 3 — Access Blocked by Missing Permission Boundary
**Setup:** - IAM Role `restricted-role` with identity policy allowing `s3:*` - Permission boundary on `restricted-role` that only allows `s3:GetObject`
**Expected:** DENY for `s3:PutObject` (blocked at Layer 4), ALLOW for `s3:GetObject`
**Key point:** identity policy alone is insufficient; both identity policy AND boundary must allow.
---
### Scenario 4 — Bucket Policy Grants Cross-Account Access (No IAM Policy Needed)
**Setup:** - IAM Role `cross-account-role` in account `111111111111` - Bucket in account `222222222222` - Bucket policy explicitly allows `cross-account-role`'s ARN
**Expected:** ALLOW via Layer 3 (bucket policy) — identity-based policy not required for same-account resources.
---
### Scenario 5 — SCP Blocks Access Org-Wide
**Setup:** - AWS Organization SCP denying `s3:DeleteBucket` for all principals - IAM Role with full `s3:*` identity policy
**Expected:** DENY at Layer 2 for `s3:DeleteBucket`
---
### Scenario 6 — Wildcard Resource in Policy
**Setup:** - IAM policy with `Resource = "*"` allowing `s3:GetObject` - Query against a specific bucket
**Expected:** ALLOW (wildcard matches all buckets)
**Test note:** transformer must expand `"*"` to include all known `Resource` sigs in the Alloy spec.
---
### Scenario 7 — Implicit Deny (No Policy Covers the Action)
**Setup:** - IAM Role with no policies attached - S3 bucket with no bucket policy
**Expected:** IMPLICIT DENY (no Allow found anywhere)
---
### Scenario 8 — Inline Policy vs Managed Policy
**Setup:** - `aws_iam_role_policy` (inline) and `aws_iam_policy` + attachment (managed) on same role - Inline policy allows `s3:GetObject`, managed policy allows `s3:PutObject`
**Expected:** both actions ALLOW (union of all attached policies)
---
## Requirements & Test Scenarios: Per-Action Access Evaluation
The system must: - For every (principal, bucket, action) triple, evaluate access according to all AWS S3 policy evaluation steps (explicit Deny, RCP, SCP, resource-based, identity-based, permission boundaries, session policies). - Correctly model explicit Deny and Allow statements from Terraform policies, including Deny for specific actions (e.g., s3:DeleteObject) and Allow for others (e.g., s3:GetObject). - Output, for each query, the final access decision (ALLOW or DENY) and the evaluation layer responsible (e.g., DENY at Layer 1 due to explicit Deny in bucket policy). - Generate Alloy assertions for every (principal, bucket, action) triple, so that Alloy checks confirm the tool's reasoning for each action.
### Example Test Scenario
Given a Terraform file with a bucket policy that Denies s3:DeleteObject and Allows s3:GetObject for a principal: - The generated Alloy model must DENY DeleteObject at Layer 1 (explicit Deny) and ALLOW GetObject if it passes all layers. - The Alloy output must show which layer made the decision and why, matching the AWS evaluation logic.
---
## Table: AWS S3 Access Control Scenarios (Terraform → Alloy)
| AWS Evaluation Step | Terraform Resource(s) | Scenario Description | Expected Alloy Model/Predicate | Example Outcome | |---------------------|----------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------|-----------------| | 1. Explicit Deny | aws_s3_bucket_policy, aws_iam_policy, aws_organizations_policy | Policy with `Effect = "Deny"` for principal/action/resource | `explicitDenyExists[p, r, a]` | DENY at Layer 1 | | 1. Explicit Deny | aws_s3_bucket_policy | No explicit Deny for action/principal | `not explicitDenyExists[p, r, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 2. RCP | aws_organizations_policy (RCP) | RCP does not allow action | `not rcpAllows[p, a]` | DENY at Layer 2 | | 2. RCP | aws_organizations_policy (RCP) | RCP allows action | `rcpAllows[p, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 3. SCP | aws_organizations_policy (SCP) | SCP does not allow action | `not scpAllows[p, a]` | DENY at Layer 3 | | 3. SCP | aws_organizations_policy (SCP) | SCP allows action | `scpAllows[p, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 4. Resource-Based | aws_s3_bucket_policy | Bucket policy allows action for principal | `bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a]` | ALLOW at Layer 4 (if principal is cross-account) | | 4. Resource-Based | aws_s3_bucket_policy | No bucket policy allows action | `not bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 5. Identity-Based | aws_iam_policy, aws_iam_role_policy, aws_iam_user_policy | Identity policy allows action | `identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 5. Identity-Based | aws_iam_policy, aws_iam_role_policy, aws_iam_user_policy | No identity policy allows action | `not identityPolicyAllows[p, r, a]` | DENY at Layer 5 | | 6. Permission Boundary | aws_iam_role (permissions_boundary), aws_iam_policy | Permission boundary allows action | `permBoundaryAllows[p, a]` | Continue to next layer | | 6. Permission Boundary | aws_iam_role (permissions_boundary), aws_iam_policy | Permission boundary does not allow action | `not permBoundaryAllows[p, a]` | DENY at Layer 6 | | 7. Session Policy | aws_iam_policy (session), sts:AssumeRoleWithPolicy | Session policy allows action | `sessionPolicyAllows[p, a]` | ALLOW at Layer 7 | | 7. Session Policy | aws_iam_policy (session), sts:AssumeRoleWithPolicy | Session policy does not allow action | `not sessionPolicyAllows[p, a]` | DENY at Layer 7 | | Implicit Deny | (any, when no Allow found) | No policy allows action at any layer | (no predicate matches) | IMPLICIT DENY | | Wildcard Action | any policy | `Action = ["*"]` or `Action = ["s3:*"]` | Alloy expands to all known actions | All actions covered | | Wildcard Resource | any policy | `Resource = ["*"]` or `Resource = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"]` | Alloy expands to all known resources | All resources covered | | Cross-Account Allow | aws_s3_bucket_policy | Bucket policy allows principal from another account | `bucketPolicyAllows[p, r, a]` | ALLOW at Layer 4 | | No Policy | (none) | No relevant policy attached | (no predicate matches) | IMPLICIT DENY |
---
## Suggested Implementation Order
``` Phase 1 — Foundation [x] Data model (model.go) [ ] Terraform parser for aws_iam_role, aws_iam_policy, aws_s3_bucket [ ] Basic Alloy template generation (no conditions)
Phase 2 — Core Analysis [ ] Full policy attachment resolution [ ] Alloy spec with all 7 evaluation layers [ ] Alloy CLI integration & output parsing
Phase 3 — Coverage [ ] IAM conditions support (StringEquals, ArnLike, etc.) [ ] aws_organizations_policy (SCP) parsing [ ] Permission boundary parsing [ ] Cross-account analysis
Phase 4 — UX [ ] JSON and SARIF output [ ] CI mode (exit code 1 on DENY findings) [ ] Example fixtures and integration tests ```
---
## Dependencies to Add to go.mod
``` github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2 v2.19.1 # HCL parsing github.com/zclconf/go-cty v1.14.1 # HCL value types github.com/hashicorp/go-multierror v1.1.1 # error aggregation github.com/stretchr/testify v1.9.0 # test assertions ```

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
